title: Cavallo Hoof Boots Australia is UP AND RUNNING!
text: Cavallo Down Under An exciting announcement! The Cavallo Hoof Boots Australia website has just gone LIVE! Cavallo Hoof Boots Australia is UP AND RUNNING! Ready to serve the Down Under Cavallo Community! With the same great service that's made Cavallo famous in North America, the UK and Europe, the new Cavallo Australia online shopping experience gives Australians quick and easy access to the full range of Cavallo products. Cavallo Hoof Boots Australia is UP AND RUNNING! So if you're in Australia, hug your horse and rejoice! Cavallo is HERE! You can now also Like and follow Cavallo Hoof Boots Australia on Facebook HERE! Wishing you many happy trails under the Australian sun,

title: So, you Want to Jump in your Cavallos?
text: A Conclusion you can Jump to So, you Want to Jump in your Cavallo Hoof Boots? Jumping is a multifaceted sport. Success requires harmonization of both horse and human bodies, individually and collectively. Rewarding when accomplished with finesse and agility. Life-threatening when uncoordinated and clumsy. Have you ever watched, in dumbfound awe, at the magic and lightness while a team flows perfectly through a complex course? Have you ever sat and wondered what in the world someone was thinking when trying to navigate a corner and direct their horse over a standard with completely inappropriate management? It’s opposite ends of the same spectrum. Same sport. Completely different delivery. Challenging both ways, and for entirely different reasons. Leaving the nuances of performance behind, lets turn to the effect jumping has on the body - primarily horses’ hooves. This is always the favorite topic at Cavallo. Concussion. Impact. Shock. Massive force of weight, descending on those relatively small hooves. [caption id="attachment_18282" align="alignleft" width="1280"] Summer Wade Jumping - Hoof Pics photography[/caption] Here’s what one foremost authority on this topic has to say: Dr. Robert Bowker, DVM - from Horse & Rider, Feb. 2006, “The blood in horses' feet does much more than provide nutrients to hoof tissues. It also enables the unshod foot to function as a hydraulic system

title: What the Cavallo CLB Boot can do for your Mini Horse
text: A Full Range of CLB Sizes Offered! Announcing a FULL RANGE of Cavallo CLB Sizes! You asked and Cavallo responded - The Cavallo CLB (Cute Little Boot) miniature horse (and VSE - Very Small Equine) hoof boot sizing range now includes size M1 all the way up to M4. AND each and every size is now available on both Slim or Regular Soles! The new size M1 is made to fit the smallest of miniature horses, while the largest CLB, the M4, is sized to fit a large miniature horse, a pony or very small regular horse hoof. The M4 is also offered to “bridge the gap” between our miniature and regular size ranges. The M1 – M4 size range now gives a complete selection of sizing, covering the smaller hoofs. We pride ourselves in our customer service. Not only are Cavallos the best hoof boots available, but we also maintain significant inventory to do the best we possibly can to avoid any back-orders. Madison Andrae and Daisie of Daisie's Crew enjoying a visit at a care home in Illinois . Bettering the Lives of Miniature Horses Over the past five years, the CLB has climbed from being a boot offered in a single size (M2) to be the World’s most recognized and popular miniature hoof boot. Keeping up with demand after the initial CLB launch was challenging. We had underestimated the need for mini boots to cater to our smaller equine friends. We progressed to offer the M3 size in 2016, and escalating dema

title: Cavallo Hoof Boots Proven to Minimize Load Impact
text: Cavallo Hoof Boots Proven to Minimize Load Impact A recent study announced that C avallo Hoof Boots have been proven to minimize load impact. Western Kentucky University recently conducted a study to determine Cavallo Trek boots ' effect on hoof pressure distribution. Science student, Gabriella Lynn, chose Trek boots precisely due to the full coverage of the hoof and its suitable tread for riding over rough ground. Ms. Lynn presented her findings at the 2019 Equine Science Society Symposium, held June 3-6 in Asheville, North Carolina. [caption id="attachment_19558" align="alignnone" width="956"] Diagram showing how Fuji Prescale Film Works. Photo credit: FujiFilm[/caption] Measuring Pressure by Using Film This new study measured pressure distribution as horses walked over Fujifilm Low Prescale Film. Prescale is a unique film tool that “reveals the distribution and magnitude of pressure between any two contacting, mating, or impacting surfaces." The study measured both impact to bare hoofs, and hoofs protected with Trek Boots. Asphalt and crushed stone were used as aggressive hard surfaces for this testing. The color film images revealed pressure over the hoof wall and sole. It also measured minimum and maximum pressure, mean pressure, size of contact area, and force. After analyzing the results, Lynn observed that hoof boots created more low-pressure regions on the film than ba

title: Taking Care of the 'Big Guys' with Cavallo BFB Draft-Sized Hoof Boots
text: Cavallo BFB 'Big Foot Boot' is a Must-Have for Large Horses [caption id="attachment_19938" align="alignleft" width="452"] BFB's and feathers go so well together! Photo: Connie Challice[/caption] By request, Cavallo BFB Draft-sized Hoof Boots were developed to meet the needs of carriage/driving, working and pleasure drafts, as well as those in need of a boot during periods of hoof rehabilitation. Producing a hoof boot that could support the weight and activity of animals weighing around a ton was no simple task for us! Technically advanced materials are selected to withstand intense weight and pressure in repetitive motion and be counted on to provide shock absorption and weight distribution. Even on hard asphalt, gravel or rocky trails, these boots perform! Offered in sizes 7, 8, 9 and 10, the success of BFB became immediately apparent. BFB Poly Pad insoles are also an option in our product line. These insoles function to provide even more shock absorption when needed or to tighten boot fit (just like with our human shoes). Supporting Draft Horses – From the Ground Up Constructed on soles of a specially formulated thermoplastic urethane (TPU) compound, BFB provides maximum protection, unsurpassed traction, durability, and abrasion resistance. Uppers made of industrial-grade 1680 Denier Nylon, with secure wrap-around closures, offer quick hoof insertion and full protection. Viol

title: Where Will Carole Be Next???
text: Where will Carole be next??? I'm hitting the road next week because I have a mission, and I have to show up to tell you about it. I need to inspire and improve our lives with horses. When I work from home I make posts, videos, and write blogs and emails to try to support you as best I can. But the best thing is coming out to be with you, in person, for real. First Stop - Close to Home! My first stop is Mane Event, Chilliwack, British Columbia (Oct. 25-27)! I'll be speaking about Laminitis and overall hoof care, and I'll be around during the show to meet you and talk about my most-loved topic – hoof boots. If you are there, please come chat at our Cavallo booth (#201). This bustling show is a yearly favorite. We can hardly wait! Greg, Jenny, and I attend. We're excited each year to see the folks from Back Country Horsemen of BC, H orse Council BC, the Okanagan School of Natural Hoof Care, and more! And we're also very excited to be meeting up with Robert Eversole, the Trailmeister. He’ll be speaking too and sharing his vast knowledge of North America's trails, and his experience as a back-country rider. During my presentations, I share my experience accumulated throughout the world with industry experts. I will present studies of hoof care, the barefoot movement, and hoof boot development, highlighting various real-life stories of riders and horses affected by this revolution ov

title: CLB is Now the Official Hoof Boot of the American Miniature Horse Association
text: Did you know the CLB is now the official hoof boot of the American Miniature Horse Association? Cavallo is proud to announce that the CLB (Cute Little Boot) Hoof Boot has been selected by the American Miniature Horse Association as the Official Hoof Boot of the AMHA! Two Passionate and Active Communities Unite I'm thrilled about this new partnership! The AMHA has long been known for their celebration of the miniature horse, and their passion for breeding, showing and driving activities. The Cavallo brand is synonymous with the appreciation of horses and the provision of equine comfort and protection. Our CLB Hoof Boot offers miniature horses full protection for all activities enjoyed by minis and their owners. It just made sense for us to come together and we couldn’t be more excited to be working with this great organization! The AMHA also recently commented on our exciting partnership: ““The American Miniature Horse Association is proud to partner with Cavallo Boots, our official horse boot!”. [caption id="attachment_20569" align="alignleft" width="495"] Megan Postletheweight and Hughie - AMHA World Champions[/caption] The Four Cornerstones of CLB Use: Safety During Therapy Work – The CLB offers therapy minis great grip on Interior floor surfaces (hospitals, care homes, etc.) Driving – Minimizes fatigue by offering shock absorption on asphalt. title: Wrestling Navicular?  Trust your Gut.
text: Wrestling Navicular? Trust your Gut. 🤠 "My vet wanted me to put egg butt shoes on with a riser wedge, due to my mates low heels. He said she was in the pre-navicular stages. Instead, I have this fantastic farrier that brought her heels up slowly with corrective trims. She has not taken a lame step in over 6 months! When I’m going out onto the trail I throw her Cavallo Boots on and GO! ❤️ Wrestling Navicular? Trust your Gut. - Deana Womack We're here to help! Want to talk to a Cavallo Hoof Boot expert about how you can help your horse? Contact us HERE! [caption id="attachment_20901" align="alignleft" width="720"] Deana stuck to her gut and followed her heart - and her best friend thanks her![/caption]

title: Cheyenne Wimberley - Heading to the National Finals Rodeo with Trek
text: Cheyenne Wimberley is Heading to the National Finals Rodeo with Trek! Cheyenne Wimberly is an accomplished barrel racer and three-time National Finals Rodeo Qualifier (WOW!)! She's heading out on the road again and will compete at the National Finals Rodeo (NFR) in Las Vegas, this December! Trailering with Trek Cheyenne recently got in touch with us. She's happy to offer her hard-working horse Cavallo Trek Hoof Boots to offer comfort and fight fatigue while trailering. Her horse enjoys Trek with Comfort Pads added (cushy pads that increase comfort on the road and reduce fatigue). Cheyenne also pops in Protection Pads (thin, Kevlar-type pads that protect the boot soles from the metal shoes). Along with the top performance her horse gives during competition, there are also many hours spent on the road in between events. Cheyenne does whatever she can to make the travel easier on her horse. We're pleased to hear that Cavallos are being used by this dynamic duo - and with great results! Back in the Saddle - to WIN! Cheyenne Wimberley trailers with Trek Hoof Boots This year's NFR is very meaningful for Cheyenne as it's been 21 years since she last qualified. Now she's officially "back in the saddle"! She's been occupied with other things over the past 21 years (including training horses for some of the USA's BEST riders!) and has now found herself back in a place where she's ready t

title: What YOU'RE Saying about Cavallo!
text: BLESSINGS TO THIS FARRIER!! "Gunner is a 10 year old Quarter horse, and he's the sweetest-natured animal I've ever had. Unfortunately, he had a previous owner who didn't care for him very much. I knew Gunner before and after the injury that changed the course of his life. Gunner with a new lease on life in his Cavallos This sweet horse got tangled in loose barbed wire that never should have been left laying around. It gashed his pastern open and he received no vet care of any kind. I didn't own him then and did what I could to help him but he needed a vet badly. The poor horse's leg swelled up like a stove pipe. When the swelling went down, he was left with a large scar and a large, hard lump on his pastern. Soon he was 3-legged-lame, hobbling around a massive pasture for months, getting beaten up by other horses. :( Gunner Gets a New Start Finally, I was able to convince his owner to sell him and I loaded him on my trailer 10 seconds after they agreed! I drove him the hour and a half to my vet. He was examined and x-rayed. Turns out the barbed wire had damaged his pastern joint and, because he was left without any medical care, he had developed arthritis. The large, hard lump on his pastern had calcified into bone and would always be there. His prognosis wasn't great. It was hard to say if he'd ever be sound enough to ride, or even sound enough to be a "pasture puff". We began

title: What is Merry Wearing on her Feet?
text: A Question I'm Often Asked... What is Merry wearing on her feet? Recently, I've received a few comments on my photographs asking about Merry's boots. The answer? Cavallo Hoof Boots! I keep Merry barefoot; it suits our lifestyle perfectly. The most important reason why? Her comfort - and the positive side effect is that she isn't ripping shoes off with parts of her hoof wall. Barefoot and No Going Back! The more I learn about keeping Merry barefoot, the more it confirms it is the right decision for us. Merry is ever so slightly pigeon toed, when she was initially shod she would rip her shoes off and we eventually had more glue than hoof. This was the first reason that I realized shoeing Merry was not right for her. Now she's barefoot her feet look fantastic. Merry has been barefoot for 99.9% of the time that I have owned her. When she arrived I had her shod. Her feet were a bit of a mess and I automatically thought having her shod was the answer. I was wrong, and unfortunately it made them worse - a lot worse. My heart would sink when she would walk into the yard, minus a shoe and lots of hoof wall missing. Looking Hard for the "Cons"... Over time, I've learnt about the anatomy of the hoof, the pros of keeping her barefoot, (I've not found any cons I have to admit) and how the foot flexes and absorbs movement. When Merry is ridden regularly over the Summer, her feet condition ni
